mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 1 Series 120i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned BMW 1 Series 120i engine costs £2,500–£5,000 supply only, varying by generation: N46 £1,800–£3,000, N43 £2,200–£3,800, N13 £2,500–£4,200, B48 £3,500–£6,500. Fitting adds £900–£1,500. Check the Era Map above for your engine code.

That depends on which engine your 120i has. The N46 is a robust but ageing naturally aspirated unit; the N43 adds direct-injection complexity; the N13 (Prince engine) has timing chain and HPFP issues pre-2015; the B48 is BMW's most refined and reliable four-cylinder. F40-generation (B48) is the best bet.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 1 Series 120i?

acceptedAnswer.text: N13: timing chain tensioner (cold-start rattle, £800–£1,500) and HPFP failure (long cranking, £600–£1,200). N43: NOx sensor failure (engine light, emissions, £400–£1,000). B48: coolant leaks and water pump issues (£400–£800). N46: VANOS seals and coil packs (£300–£800).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 1 Series 120i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: 1 Series values range from £2,000–£25,000 depending on generation, condition and mileage. A £2,500–£5,000 reconditioned engine represents 20–80% of vehicle value — our 70% BMW scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained F40, replacement is often justified. For an ageing E8x with other issues, assess scrap value first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 1 Series 120i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 120i has used four different engines: N46B20 (2004–2007, 150bhp), N43B20 (2007–2011, 170bhp), N13B16 (2011–2019, 170–184bhp), and B48B20 (2019–present, 184–190bhp). Check your VIN or registration year. The code is stamped on the engine block.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N13 timing chain issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. Timing chain and tensioner replacement costs £800–£1,500 and is significantly cheaper than a full engine replacement (£2,200–£4,200). The job requires specialist knowledge of the Prince engine. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately — ignored chain wear can lead to catastrophic piston-valve contact.

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 1 Series 120i engine typically takes 8–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent BMW specialist) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The N13's turbo adds complexity vs N46/N43.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW 1 Series 120i — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches the fault before catastrophic failure
Straightforward repair on an otherwise healthy engine
Viable — MOT pass depends on NOx/emissions compliance
Assess vehicle value carefully — see scrap threshold

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW 1 Series 120i? Check These First
buyIf: * F40-generation B48 (2019+) — the most reliable 120i by a margin
* N13 (F20) post-2015 with timing chain history and HPFP replaced
* N46 with documented VANOS seal and coil pack history
* Full service history with correct oil specification for the era
avoidIf: * N13 pre-2015 with no timing chain history — the risk is real
* N43 with emissions warning light and no diagnosis (NOx sensor likely)
* Any 120i with poor service history — all engines are unforgiving of neglect
* E8x N46 with no VANOS history — simple to fix but suggests neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 120i is a badge that spans 20 years of BMW four-cylinder engineering, from the robust but ageing N46 to the sophisticated B48. The F40 B48 is the most reliable, the N13 is the most temperamental (but can be made reliable), and the N46 remains a solid budget choice if maintained. Buy on history, maintain the chain/HPFP on the N13, and the 120i will serve you well.

cta.label: → Compare BMW 1 Series 120i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 1 Series 120i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Any rattle? N13 chain rattle is metallic; N46/N43 should be quiet
* Service History — Check the year to identify which engine — pre-2007 N46, 2007-2011 N43, 2011-2019 N13, 2019+ B48
* Timing Chain History — N13 (pre-2015): chains replaced? Tensioner checked?
* High-Pressure Fuel Pump — N13: has HPFP been replaced? Long cranking is a warning
* Emissions / NOx — N43: any engine light? NOx sensor is common
* VANOS Operation — N46: check low-end power; VANOS seals degrade
* Coolant Level — B48: any loss? Water pump and connections are key risks
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Resolve before buying
